item.title: Johannesburg Struggles to Keep Lights on But Repays Bondholders
item.description: The City of Johannesburg, which is on the verge of financial collapse, put investors at the front of its queue of creditors this week when it repaid a 1.44 billion rand ($88 million) bond.

item.title: OpenAI Weighs IPO in 2027 After Expected Anthropic Public Debut
item.description: OpenAI is considering an initial public offering as soon as in 2027, according to people familiar with the matter, a timeline that would potentially see it go public after its artificial intelligence rival Anthropic PBC.

item.title: China Three Gorges Is Said In Talks to Buy German Wind Assets
item.description: China Three Gorges Corp. is in talks to buy a German wind portfolio from NEAG Norddeutsche Energie AG, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: AI Is Already Reshaping US Politics at Every Level
item.description: From data center backlash to boundless cash from tech billionaires and concerns about deepfaked campaign ads, AI is everywhere in the 2026 US elections.

item.title: Cantor Bitcoin SPAC Lets Investors Commit Less as Crypto Slides
item.description: A blank-check company associated with Cantor Fitzgerald is trying to get investors to support an upcoming crypto deal by allowing them commit less money than they had initially pledged.

item.title: Disney’s Parks Revamp Includes Aquatic Robots and Hovering Star Wars Droids
item.description: Inside a Walt Disney Co. research and development lab, supported by some heavy-duty scaffolding, sits a wooden manta ray prototype roughly the size of a car. If all goes according to plan, the final version will be able to transform into the Gramma Tala character from Moana, used at a lagoon in a Disney park sometime soon. Down the line, the company will add a fleet of dolphin-like robots to the experience.

item.title: Legendary Japanese Game Director Returns With ‘Gen ATLAS’
item.description: Fumito Ueda, director of Ico and Shadow of the Colossus, is back with a new game after more than a decade

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Rises But Remains Close to Record Low
item.description: US consumer sentiment rose in June as lower gasoline prices provided some relief for Americans grappling with high inflation.

item.title: US Natural Gas Rises on Hot Forecasts as July Contract Expires
item.description: US natural gas futures increased in the final day of trading for the July contract as forecasts continued to show above-average temperatures in the first week of July. Individual transactions moved the market more amid a drop-off in liquidity near expiry.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Is Ramping Up Oil Exports as Gulf Ports Restart
item.description: Saudi Arabia is boosting crude shipments, opening up its Persian Gulf ports that were shuttered by the Iran war while also increasing loadings at Red Sea outlets that it has used to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Denmark’s EIFO Invests €200 Million in EU Fund for Tech Scale-Up
item.description: Denmark’s Export and Investment Fund has committed €200 million to the €5 billion Scaleup Europe Fund, becoming the only state-backed anchor investor in a vehicle designed to help build Europe’s next generation of technology champions.

item.title: US Goods Trade Deficit Widens to Biggest in More Than a Year
item.description: The US merchandise-trade deficit widened in May to the biggest in more than a year as exports fell and imports rose.

item.title: Bed Bath & Beyond Nabs Bull On ‘Everything Home’ Transformation
item.description: Bed Bath & Beyond, Inc. gained a new bull this week who sees upside for shares on the premise that it will soon be known as a one-stop shop for home improvement and financing services as well as its pillows and pots.

item.title: Bosch CEO Unexpectedly Leaves World’s Biggest Car-Parts Maker
item.description: Robert Bosch GmbH Chief Executive Officer Stefan Hartung is stepping down after leading a push to cut thousands of jobs and make the world’s biggest automotive supplier more competitive.

item.title: Bitcoin Bottom Hunters Fear Fresh Pain After $1.3 Trillion Rout
item.description: Bitcoin’s collapse is forcing crypto veterans to confront the question every bear market eventually asks: when does mass panic create a buying opportunity? The answer, according to many of the investors and analysts who have lived through previous boom-and-bust cycles, is: not yet.

item.title: Bond Traders Stunned as Losses on SpaceX’s New Debt Keep Growing
item.description: SpaceX’s blockbuster bond sale is weakening so quickly in the secondary market that traders say they can’t recall another recent deal that widened this sharply.

item.title: Hungary to Meet Euro Entry Criteria by 2030, Premier Magyar Says
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar said his country could meet the European Union’s economic criteria for adopting the euro by 2030, setting out the clearest timetable yet for a path toward the common currency.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 26, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.5% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York as weakness in chip stocks resumes.

item.title: Goldman Strategist Sees Bets in Big Tech During Chip Volatility
item.description: Big tech stocks could turn more attractive in the artificial intelligence trade as chipmakers show continued volatility, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. strategist Christian Mueller-Glissmann.

item.title: Boeing Wins Jet Deal From China Southern Valued at $3.62 Billion
item.description: Boeing Co. secured an order from China Southern Airlines Co. valued at $3.62 billion, marking an important win for the US manufacturer after seeing orders dry up from Asia’s largest aviation market in the past decade.

item.title: Lebanon’s 400% Bond Rally Fades as War Wrecks Debt Recovery Math
item.description: A 400% rally in Lebanon’s defaulted bonds is fading as investors confront the prospect of debt restructuring delays and far lower recovery rates than they had expected.

item.title: BOE Sees Weather as New Inflation Risk While London Sizzles
item.description: As London bakes in record-breaking sunshine, Bank of England officials are beginning to fret that, as one supply shock dissipates, the weather could produce the next one to push up inflation.

item.title: Solar and Batteries Are Coming for Gas’s Crown in Texas Power
item.description: Fossil fuels still rule the Lone Star State, but renewable energy is catching up.

item.title: OpenAI Makes Its Advertising Pitch at Cannes Festival
item.description: The ChatGPT maker offers the industry a new marketing vehicle

item.title: EU’s €800 Billion Splurge Sees Results in Naples
item.description: What happened when the European Union splurged €800 billion on its economy? Take a look at Naples.

item.title: Nigeria Draws First Tranche of $5 Billion Swap With UAE Bank
item.description: Nigeria has accessed the first tranche of a $5 billion derivatives deal with United Arab Emirates’ largest lender, pressing ahead with a transaction that has been scrutinized for being opaque.

item.title: Washington’s Foreign Ban on Anthropic’s Top Models May Backfire
item.description: The move may act as a tailwind for rival China, boosting demand for the Asian nation’s open source AI.

item.title: AI Is Splitting Tech Stocks Into Winners and Losers
item.description: This week’s stock moves show that the AI rally has stopped being a single trade.

item.title: Egypt Asset Sales Ease Path Toward $1.6 Billion in IMF Funding
item.description: Egypt’s recent state-asset sales have satisfied the targets of an International Monetary Fund review, according to people familiar with the matter, easing the path to unlock about $1.6 billion in financing for an economy buffeted by the Iran war.

item.title: Metinvest Seeks New Investor for Italy’s Landmark Steel Project
item.description: Metinvest Holding is looking for a new investor to provide equity financing for a €3 billion ($3.4 billion) steel plant in Italy as the Ukrainian group tries to downsize its commitment.
